title: US PPI Forecast Snapshot
summary: May PPI surprised hot headline (+1.1% MoM, 6.5% YoY) on gasoline +23.4%, but core came in softer than feared at +0.4% MoM (4.9% YoY) vs 0.5% consensus. Services slowed to +0.3% from 0.7%. June energy PPI collapses as WTI fell from $91+ to $72.6 after the Hormuz deal. Headline likely flat to slightly positive; core stays sticky around +0.3%. The Fed watches core, not the energy mirage.
baseCase: Flat headline, sticky core; energy mean-reversion masks upstream persistence

release.name: PPI
release.thesis: Energy PPI mean-reverts after Hormuz reopening and WTI collapse to $72.6. Core came in softer than feared in May (+0.4% vs 0.5% consensus) and services slowed. June core stays sticky around +0.3% on lagged petrochemical passthrough and portfolio management. The headline is an energy story; core is the signal.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.1%, core +0.3%. Energy mean-reverts, core stays sticky but not accelerating.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Rates digest the headline but focus on core. No major Fed repricing. Cyclicals stabilize if margin narrative holds. Treasury yields drift lower.
scenarios.invalidation: Core services re-accelerate above +0.5% MoM or headline surprises above +0.4%.

scenarios.label: Hotter pipeline pressure
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.5%, core +0.5%. Lagged passthrough hits harder than expected; services stay hot.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Rates higher, USD firmer, margin-sensitive equities under pressure. Fed hawkishness extends on core stickiness.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print is driven by narrow commodity volatility with weak pass-through into CPI or margins.

scenarios.label: Cleaner disinflation pulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline -0.2%, core +0.2%. Energy collapses further; core passthrough fades faster than expected.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Fed-cut odds rise, rates soften, broader equity appetite improves. Margin relief narrative strengthens.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced a benign outcome before release or oil snaps back on Hormuz deal collapse.

setups.bias: Long duration if core PPI cools without hawkish spillover
setups.trigger: Core PPI <= +0.2% MoM and revisions are benign
setups.invalidation: Broad upstream re-acceleration with hawkish rates repricing

setups.bias: Constructive only if margin pressure eases
setups.trigger: Core goods and services pipeline both avoid upside surprise; May core softness confirmed
setups.invalidation: Input cost shock restarts (Hormuz deal collapses, energy spikes above $85)

setups.bias: Long on hotter PPI surprise with rates follow-through
setups.trigger: Headline and core both beat with higher terminal-rate pricing
setups.invalidation: Rates fade despite the beat or oil collapse drives risk-on

watchItems.label: Final demand services
watchItems.note: May services slowed to +0.3% from 0.7%. A re-acceleration above +0.5% would flip the narrative.

watchItems.label: Commodity complex
watchItems.note: WTI $72.6 (June 24) is below pre-conflict levels. If it holds, energy PPI collapses. Watch Hormuz deal fragility.

watchItems.label: Pass-through
watchItems.note: May core +0.4% was softer than feared. Focus on whether June confirms the trend or reverses it.

keyDrivers: May core PPI came in softer than consensus (+0.4% vs 0.5%). Services slowed to +0.3% from 0.7%.
Oil mean-reverts after Hormuz deal: WTI $91+ -> $72.6. Energy PPI collapses in June.
PPI matters most when it shifts margin expectations or the CPI narrative.
60-day Hormuz deal window is fragile. Collapse risk re-energizes energy PPI.
risks: May headline PPI was very hot (+1.1% MoM). Revisions can dominate the first market read.
Hormuz deal is 60-day and fragile. Israeli action in Lebanon or Iran non-compliance can collapse it.
Macro tape can be driven by yields or oil even if PPI itself is tame.
Core services may re-accelerate if portfolio management and healthcare pricing stay sticky.
